notes: The game starts in borderless mode. But when loading from a saved their characters are not displayed either on the map or on the interface. At the beginning of a new game, the characters are visible, after loading there is no. All NPCs are visible.
title: Kenshi

notes: The game ran well for me both in fullscreen and borderless fullscreen. I did not configure anything special through winecfg, only disabled esync. I had to disable hardware cursor in the options to get the ingame mouse cursor to show. This is important because the cursor is context sensitive in game.
title: Kenshi

notes:
Out of the box, the game always crashed when starting a new game. I saw that it works for some people so I tried some tweaks and it turns out with PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 it works great for me!

I checked wether my system was correctly set up for ESYNC, and it is, so I wonder why the game does not work for some people when ESYNC is enabled.

I also read about the game having issues with Fullscreen, but I played both windowed and borderless fullscreen without issues. Did not try "normal" fullscreen.

Overall everything now works with good performance.
title: Kenshi

notes: No tweaks, fresh install of Mint Cinammon with standard closed-source Nvidia drivers from the Mint update manager.
title: Kenshi

notes:
Starts and is playable just by using SteamPlay. Not sure about performance compared to Windows, but the game regularly hops between ~90 and ~20 fps (It might be same on win). It's not a big issue though since high and smooth framerate is not required in this game as you have pause and multiple speed options. (Played it for 22 hours now and haven't been too bothered by the performance).


Haven't had any crashes, but I did encounter some bugs. (All town inhabitants disappeared, probably a game bug).


So all is good as far as ease of use and setup is concerned but Silver due to the framerate issues as I wouldn't call that running perfectly. (Runs with minor issues, but is generally playable).


PS: I do have the NOFILE limit increased.
title: Kenshi

notes:
Disable Esync + borderless = Works. Been playing it on an i7 + onboard gpu machine with Windows and got more stability. On the other hand, both versions crashed after travelling in 2 groups. Linux version grows slower and slower until the mouse becomes completely laggy (Using the keyboard to rotate the camera still works smoothly though). Shift+F11 doesn't clear the problem. In Windows, for comparison, this doesn't happen but the game is liable to crash after a while.

Note - Crashes less if staying in one area. The more of the map is travelled, the higher the chances are for crashes.
title: Kenshi

notes: Runs about as well as the Windows version. I had to launch with Stream (Runtime) and set the options to windowed (fullscreen)
title: Kenshi

notes: Borderless Window works better than fullscreen. The game is Borked without PROTON_NO_ESYNC so make sure to use that. Performance is really good, I play at maximum settings (all the sliders to max) with water reflections and shadows off and I get 60 fps in most of the places I have been (big cities and forests included). Also, shadows kill performance making the game run at 30 or so FPS even with very short draw distance and quality. Another weird thing is that the VSYNC seems to be forced on and double buffered so framerate drops will get the game stuck to 30 fps even if it could run better. Overall none of these problems impact gameplay though which is why I'm rating it Gold
title: Kenshi

notes: Works perfectly with out of the box xubuntu. Developers recommend borderless mode for better performance. Saving, loading starting, mods, everything works fine. Performance is comparable to Windows, ie, not great but definitely playable. I did notice more loading stutters on Linux compared to Windows.
title: Kenshi

notes: Runs fine through proton, even with about 20 mods installed. Only issue is with loading through different areas, where staggering occurs - but this happens on windows as well. Turn gfx down to just above potato and install the mod that compresses textures. It's not meant to be a pretty game, it's 12 years old.
